one_liner: LinkedIn Ads (Marketing/Advertising API) lets developers manage Campaign Manager ad accounts, campaigns, creatives, and analytics via a REST API secured with OAuth 2.0, but full access requires LinkedIn's manual developer vetting process.

auth_methods.notes: 3-legged OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow; requires r_ads/rw_ads scopes granted via LinkedIn Developer Portal app; access token sent as Bearer token plus a Linkedin-Version header on each request.

self_serve_notes: Developers can self-register an app in the LinkedIn Developer Portal and get 'Development tier' access (unlimited read, up to 5 ad accounts) without payment, but this is gated by LinkedIn's app review/vetting for rw_ads scope. Scaling beyond dev tier (Standard tier) requires a manual support-ticket application and approval — no fully self-serve, instant, unrestricted access. No paid public pricing tier exists; it's a free but access-controlled program.

api_surface.notes: Covers ad accounts, account users, campaign groups, campaigns, creatives, targeting, and analytics/reporting endpoints, versioned via Linkedin-Version header (e.g., https://api.linkedin.com/rest/adAccountUsers).

main_blocker: Access to write scopes (rw_ads) and scaling beyond the 5-account Development tier requires LinkedIn's manual app review/vetting and a support-ticket approval for Standard tier, so full self-serve automation is gated by LinkedIn approval rather than purely technical means.

evidence.supports: OAuth 2.0 with rw_ads/r_ads scopes required; dev tier limited to 5 ad accounts
Access tiers (Development vs Standard) and approval process
Access token generation process via OAuth
Broad REST API surface covering ad accounts, campaigns, creatives, analytics
Manual vetting/support ticket needed to increase access beyond development tier
